channel.title: Posts on Dario Arcos-Díaz, PhD
channel.description: Recent content in Posts on Dario Arcos-Díaz, PhD

item.title: Building a multi-agent system for drug target discovery
item.description: I built a multi-agent system in plain Python that takes a disease name and autonomously finds potential drug targets by querying public bioinformatics databases. It matched real-world pharma consensus on Alzheimer's, Parkinson's, and schizophrenia.

item.title: Classifying brain regions from gene expression RNA-seq data
item.description: I trained an XGBoost classifier on GTEx bulk RNA-seq to distinguish 13 brain regions. It got 95% accuracy, and the top identified genes are also known tissue markers in neuroscience.

item.title: Graph Convolutional Networks for Fraud Detection of Bitcoin Transactions
item.description: Detecting fraudulent transactions is essential in keeping financial systems trustworthy. Here I illustrate an end-to-end approach of node classification by graph neural networks to identify suspicious transactions.

item.title: Personalized Medicine Kaggle Competition
item.description: This was my approach to the Personalized Healthcare Redefining Cancer Treatment Kaggle competition. The goal of the competition was to create a machine learning algorithm that can classify genetic variations that are present in cancer cells.
